steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student claims people have mixed feelings about cultured meat that may block adoption. The needed quotation must show both a positive view of cultured meat AND reluctance to actually consume it — that internal contradiction is the "mixed feelings" barrier.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• ""Consumers tend to believe that using less packaging when selling meat products in stores would have a significant effect on the environment."": Addresses packaging, not cultured meat at all. No mixed feelings shown. ✗
  • ""Many consumers who were surveyed believed that cultured meat would be good for the environment if widely available, but few of those same consumers were willing to try eating cultured meat themselves."": Directly shows the contradiction: positive belief about environmental benefit paired with personal unwillingness to eat it. That's exactly "mixed feelings" as a barrier. ✓
  • ""During the process of creating lab-grown meat, cells are kept at the typical body temperatures of cows and other animals in order to speed up growth."": Describes a production detail; says nothing about consumer attitudes. ✗
  • ""Advocates of lab-grown meat claim that it's better for the environment than conventional meat because it requires less land."": One-sided advocacy claim; no reluctance or mixed feeling present. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
